title: The Pragmatic Programmer: Your Journey to Mastery
author: David Thomas, Andrew Hunt
subtitle: Your Journey to Mastery
originalTitle: The Pragmatic Programmer: From Journeyman to Master

authors.name: David Thomas
authors.bio: Computer programmer, author, and editor. Studied computer science at Imperial College London. Coined the terms 'Code Kata' and 'DRY' (Don't Repeat Yourself). Original signatory of the Agile Manifesto. Co-founded the Pragmatic Bookshelf publishing company.

authors.name: Andrew Hunt
authors.bio: Software developer, author, and musician. One of the 17 original authors of the Agile Manifesto. Co-founded the Pragmatic Bookshelf with Dave Thomas. Wrote his first commercial program in 1981. Active musician playing trumpet, flugelhorn, and keyboards.

verdict: One of the most influential software engineering books ever written. Its principles have aged remarkably well over two decades. While some specific examples show their age, the core philosophy of craftsmanship, responsibility, and practical thinking remains indispensable for every developer.
